sanskrit:
दैवी संपद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता।
मा श‍ुचः संपदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव॥
transliteration: daivī sampad vimokṣāya nibandhāyāsurī matā | mā śucaḥ sampadaṁ daivīm abhijāto 'si pāṇḍava ||
literal_meaning: The divine endowment leads to liberation; the demonic one is deemed for bondage. Do not grieve—you are born with the divine endowment, O Pāṇḍava.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that his core identity is not the anxious, self-doubting voice but the innate disposition toward clarity, duty, and liberation. The verse reframes moral conflict as a diagnostic tool: divine traits (self-monitoring, empathy, principled action) move one toward freedom; demonic traits (rigid entitlement, instrumental cruelty, denial of ambivalence) tighten the psychic chains. Arjuna’s very capacity to agonize over harming his elders is proof of the divine sample within him.
psychological_state: Post-validation integration—anxiety subsides as the self-concept shifts from 'potential wrong-doer' to 'duty-bound protector'.
trigger_scenario: A whistle-blower paralyzed by fear of betraying colleagues is reminded that the sleepless nights and moral anguish themselves signal integrity, not corruption.
anchor_text: Your inner struggle itself proves you carry the light that leads to freedom.
